Summary: This study proposes three SVM classifiers using pre- and post-contrast T2 FLAIR and T1WI subtraction to differentiate treatment-related effects (TRE) from glioma recurrence. The results showed that SVM based on a combination of T1WI and T2 FLAIR subtraction maps was superior to the sole use of T1WI or T2 FLAIR for differentiating TRE from recurrence.

Condition1.reason: The study includes manual segmentation of 2D subtraction maps with visible enhancement lesions.

Condition2.reason: The study mentions manual segmentation performed by neuroradiologists, indicating that the segmentation is not fully automated.

Condition3.reason: The study extracts radiomic features from the segmented regions of interest (ROIs) using PyRadiomics, which includes hand-crafted features like Shape, First Order, GLCM, GLZSM, GLRLM, NGTDM, and GLDM.

Condition4.reason: The study uses radiomic features extracted from the images, which are then organized in a table for further analysis and model building.

Condition5.reason: The study uses SVM, which is not an end-to-end deep learning approach. The features are extracted first and then fed into the SVM classifier.

Item1.reason: The study does not explicitly mention adherence to any specific radiomics or machine learning checklists or guidelines.

Item2.reason: The study defines inclusion and exclusion criteria for patient selection, aiming to create a representative study population.

Item3.reason: The reference standard includes pathological confirmation of recurrence by second surgical resections and clinical diagnosis criteria for TRE based on stability, MRI/MRS/MRP findings, and follow-up coverage.

Item4.reason: The study involves data from two different centers, indicating a multi-center approach.

Item5.reason: The imaging data source is conventional T1WI and T2 FLAIR, which are widely used and easily accessible imaging methods for glioma diagnosis and clinical follow-up.

Item6.reason: The study reports the image acquisition protocol, including scanner details, sequence types, voxel size, FOV, contrast agent, and timing of post-contrast scans.

Item7.reason: The study includes patients with newly appeared or enlarged lesions during follow-ups, and the clinical diagnosis criteria for TRE include a minimum 6-month follow-up coverage, suggesting an appropriate interval between imaging and reference standard.

Item8.reason: The study describes the manual segmentation process performed by two neuroradiologists using ITK-SNAP, with scrutiny by a neuroradiology professor.

Item9.reason: The study involves manual segmentation, not fully automated segmentation.

Item10.reason: The final ROIs were segmented by reader 1 after ICC testing.

Item11.reason: The study mentions co-registration of pre- and post-contrast images using SPM8 and normalization of images by centering at the mean intensity and dividing by one standard deviation.

Item12.reason: The study uses PyRadiomics for feature extraction, which is a standardized software.

Item13.reason: The study describes the classes of features extracted (Shape, First Order, GLCM, GLZSM, GLRLM, NGTDM, and GLDM) and mentions the use of default parameters in PyRadiomics.

Item14.reason: The study removes features based on Pearson correlation coefficient to reduce dimensionality and redundancy.

Item15.reason: The study uses recursive feature elimination (RFE) to select the most important features for model building.

Item16.reason: The study performs feature selection to reduce the number of features and avoid overfitting, given the relatively small sample size.

Item17.reason: The study does not use an end-to-end deep learning pipeline.

Item18.reason: The study randomly splits the dataset into training and testing sets at a 7:3 ratio.

Item19.reason: The study attempts to avoid multicenter MRI standardization bias by enrolling patients who underwent similar scan setups on similar Siemens scanners.

Item20.reason: The study reports AUC, accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, PPV, and NPV to evaluate the performance of the classifiers.

Item21.reason: The study calculates 95% confidence intervals (CI) using a non-parametric bootstrap method.

Item22.reason: The study does not explicitly mention calibration assessment of the final model.

Item23.reason: The study compares the performance of SVM classifiers based on single T1WI subtraction features, single T2 FLAIR subtraction features, and a combination of both, justifying the need for a multi-parametric approach.

Item24.reason: The study compares the performance of the proposed method with previous studies based on advanced MRI sequences and conventional MRI observation.

Item25.reason: The study does not compare the performance with a simple baseline reference model.

Item26.reason: The model is tested on an independent data set that is sampled from the same source as the training and validation sets.

Item27.reason: The study includes data from two different hospitals in China, indicating external testing.

Item28.reason: The study does not mention sharing any imaging, segmentation, clinical, or radiomics analysis data with the public.

Item29.reason: The study does not mention sharing any scripts related to automatic segmentation and/or modeling with the public.

Item30.reason: The study does not mention sharing the final model in any form with the public.
